CITY OF RIO VISTA

One Main Street        P.O. Box 745        RIO VISTA, CALIFORNIA 94571

September 20, 1991

COUNCIL
707-374-6451
707-374-6217 FAX

MAYOR
Jerry Rubier

MAYOR PRO TEMPORE
Grace Anderson

Arnold Jeffery
Helen Madore
Harold Mitchell

CITY MANAGER

707-374-2930

707-374-6450

707-374-6451

Gary Bogert
707-374-6417

Larry Smyres
707-374-6451

POLICE CHIEF

707-374-6400

707-374-2323

COM. DEV. ADMIN.
Donald Sulbach
707-374-6770

PUBLIC WORKS SUPT.
Donald DeSilva
707-374-6742

TREATMENT PLANT
City Manager
707-374-2930

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Sir:

The Solano County Board of Supervisors and the Mayors of the
seven cities in Solano County have formed the organization called
the Solano City - County Coordinating Council. (See attached
agreement). This Council consists of the Mayor of each City and
the members of the Board of Supervisors who will meet each month
for one hour before the monthly Solano Water Agency meeting.

The stated purpose of the Council, as set out in the orga-
nizing agreement, is to:

discuss, coordinate, and resolve city/County issues
including but not necessarily limited land use planning,
duplication of services/improving efficiency and re-
spending effectively to the actions of other levels of
government, including the state and Federal government.
It is understood that this organization will not have
regulatory powers and that major policy decisions will
still rest with the respective City Councils and the Board
of Supervisors. However, the purpose is to provide a
regular forum to discuss matters of common interest.

Since the Council is essentially a "discussion group"
without any regulatory or decision making ability, is it
necessary for the members to file a Form 721, Statement
of Economic Interest?

Please let me know as soon as possible, your position
on this matter. If you have any question, or need more informa-
tion, do not hesitate to call.

Sincerely,

JOSEPH MULLOY
City Manager
Secretary, Solano County
Mayors Conference

Agreement

The County of Solano and the seven cities in Solano County hereby
agree that the County Board of Supervisors and the Mayors of the
seven cities will meet on a regular basis as the Solano City-County
Coordinating Council. The meetings will be held at the Solano
Irrigation District Office, 508 Elmira Road, Vacaville, California
and will occur either before or following the adjournment of the
Solano County Water Agency's monthly meeting which is held on the
second Thursday of each month at 7:00 p.m. The meetings will have
a prepared agenda which will be set by the Chairman of the Board of
Supervisors and the Chairman of the Mayor's Conference, be properly
noticed and will be open to the public. The purpose of the Council
is to discuss, coordinate, and resolve City/County issues including
but not necessarily limited to land use planning, duplication of
services/improving efficiency and responding effectively to the
actions of other levels of government, including the State and
Federal government. It is understood that this organization will
not have regulatory powers and that major policy decisions will
still rest with the respective City Councils and the Board of
Supervisors. However, the purpose herein is to provide a regular
forum to discuss matters of common interest.

[signatures]
County Board of Supervisors          City of Rio Vista

City of Benicia                       City of Suisun

City of Fairfield                     City of Vacaville

City of Dixon                         City of Vallejo
